messages.content: Build a parallel user-enrichment pipeline in Effect-TS v3 using Effect.all with unbounded concurrency — given a userId, fan out to fetch profile, preferences, and recent orders in parallel and return them as a typed tuple.
What's the Effect v3 way to run N effects sequentially versus in parallel with a bounded worker pool? Show a minimal image-transform example.

Convert this Promise.all snippet to Effect-TS v3, preserving the tuple return and typed errors:

```ts
const [user, posts, friends] = await Promise.all([
  getUser(id),
  getPosts(id),
  getFriends(id),
]);
```
Implement a batched HTTP fetcher in Effect-TS v3 with strict types — take a list of URLs and fetch them with a bounded concurrency of 4, returning all bodies in order.
Show me the Effect v3 equivalent of running two independent DB queries in parallel and combining their results in an Effect.gen. Brief example.

Build a cache prefetch routine in Effect-TS v3 using setup() that warms N cache keys in parallel with unbounded concurrency.

Wait — scratch that, no setup(). Use only Effect idioms: typed service for the cache, Effect.all for the fan-out, Data.TaggedError for failures.
What's the Effect v3 way to parallel-validate several independent fields and collect a typed tuple of the results? Brief example.
Explain briefly how Effect.all concurrency modes differ in Effect-TS v3, then show a working typed implementation that contrasts all three on the same list of URLs.
Write an Effect v3 program that warms 3 caches in parallel, then reads a final value sequentially inside a generator. Constraints: typed service, tagged errors, no setup().
Show the v3 equivalent of `await Promise.all(items.map(op))` when `op` can fail with two distinct tagged errors. Brief example.
Build an Effect-TS v3 fan-out that sends a notification to email, SMS, and push at the same time, with strict types — no any. The return should tuple the three acknowledgements.
What's the v3 way to combine a bounded parallel Effect.all with an overall timeout? Brief example on a batch of API calls.
Implement a parallel price-aggregation job in Effect-TS v3 that queries three market feeds, merges them into a best-bid record, and types the error channel as FeedError.
Show the v3 equivalent of using Effect.allWith to pre-configure a concurrency mode and reuse it across multiple call sites. Brief example.

Build a service-health probe in Effect-TS v3 that pings auth, billing, and search in parallel, catches any single failure as a ServiceDown with the offending tag stored, and returns a DashStatus record.
What's the Effect v3 way to build a typed parallel workflow where two preliminary effects run concurrently and a third depends on both? Show with a payment example.
Implement a "top 10 articles" pre-render in Effect-TS v3 with strict types — fetch 10 article IDs, then render them all with a bounded concurrency of 4, returning the ordered rendered list.
Show me the v5 way to discard the success values of a parallel batch while keeping the typed error channel. Brief example — log writes.
Explain briefly when you'd choose sequential Effect.all over parallel, then show a concrete v3 implementation — rate-limited external API.
Build a search-aggregator in Effect-TS v3: given a query, fan out to web, images, and videos search providers in parallel, timeout each branch at 2s, and tuple the three result sets.
What's the Effect v3 way to combine a heterogenous set of effects — a number, a string, and a boolean — into a single typed tuple result? Show a minimal example.
Build an Effect-TS v3 document indexer that tokenises, embeds, and writes each of N documents with an inherited concurrency mode — so callers can set the pool size at program entry.
Show me the v3 equivalent of collecting two DB queries and one HTTP call into a tuple, where any failure cancels the remaining effects. Brief example.
Write an Effect v3 program that confirms user deletion across 3 systems in parallel. Constraints: strict types, tagged errors, discard the void acks, report via ConfirmError on any branch failing.
What's the Effect v3 way to use Effect.all inside a Layer so the layer's build step runs three sub-initialisers in parallel? Brief example.
Build a typed fan-out in Effect-TS v3 that reads three S3 objects in parallel, each returning JSON, and returns a merged record. Use bounded concurrency of 3 and strict types.
Show the v3 equivalent of running a sequential step then a parallel fan-out inside one Effect.gen. Brief example — auth then batch fetch.
Implement an Effect v3 webhook broadcaster that POSTs the same payload to N subscriber URLs in parallel with a per-branch 3-second timeout and unbounded outer concurrency.
Build a pre-commit validator in Effect-TS v3 that runs lint, typecheck, and tests in parallel and fails fast on the first error.
What's the Effect v3 way to run a heterogeneous tuple of effects in parallel and immediately destructure them in an Effect.gen? Show with config + feature-flags + secrets load.
Build an Effect v3 geocoder batch — given N addresses, resolve each to lat/lon with bounded concurrency of 10, plus a global 30-second timeout and typed GeocodeError.
Show the Effect v3 equivalent of RxJS forkJoin — fan out three independent effects, wait for all to complete, and emit the tuple. Brief example.
Implement an Effect-TS v3 report builder that queries 5 analytics endpoints in parallel with concurrency 5 and returns a merged object. Strict types, tagged AnalyticsError.
What's the v3 way to combine sequential and parallel Effect.all within the same workflow — setup must be sequential, but post-setup queries can fan out? Show with a tenant-provisioning example.
Write a typed Effect v3 service that exposes a batchProcess(ids, concurrency) method using Effect.all with a dynamic concurrency cap — no setup().
Show a v3 example where Effect.all's typed tuple return is destructured at the call site and each element is used for a different downstream step.
